violations.text: Two plumbing pipes were observed leaking into a bucket. Physical facilities must be in good repair and proper adjustment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

Protective shielding is not provided for the wine room. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2023

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Service was called during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Various prepared foods was observed held at 42-52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, all foods placed in prep unit were recently prepared. All potentially hazardous foods were relocated to other refrigeration at time of inspection. Prep unit ambient temperature 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Prep unit was taken out of service during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

A hose is connected to the faucet at the dish area. This faucet is lacking a backflow prevention device. An approved backflow prevention device is required in order to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023
Two plumbing pipes were observed leaking into a bucket. Physical facilities must be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the wine room. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023
